# Transcrição: 04-ACTIVITY- Find Your Personal Story

- **Fonte:** 04-ACTIVITY- Find Your Personal Story
- **Duração:** 02:56
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 02:56]

Okay, now that you know what a story is, let's find a personal story so that you can begin practicing all of the skills that I'm going to be teaching you. I want you to first take a look at all the stories that you sort of had in mind, personal stories that you've been telling all your life, or maybe a personal story you've been waiting to tell for a long time. Figure out which one is actually a story. Maybe you've been telling a story all your life, but it's not really a story. It's a crazy thing that happened to you that doesn't actually have any change over time. Those are fine. Those are wonderful ways to entertain people. We call those romps. Sometimes we call them anecdotes if they're short enough. They're just moments from our lives that are worth sharing. You want to tell your buddies, you want to tell your friends. I tell those stories all the time on the golf course. I just know that those aren't the stories that are going to reside in someone's heart and mind. If I want to change someone's attitude, someone's belief, if I want to convince them of something, it can't be through one of those stories. Take a moment and look at your stories. How many of them are actually stories? Figure out which ones you've been telling all your life that don't really qualify. It's fine to keep telling them. Many of the strategies I'm going to teach you will actually apply to those stories as well. What I want you to do right now is to find an actual story that you're going to work on. Ideally, make it something you've never told. One of those stories that you've never gotten around to, or maybe one of those stories you've been too nervous to tell. For me, I was once arrested and tried for the crime I didn't commit. I've never told the trial story, which my storytelling friends think is crazy. I stood trial for something I didn't do, and I have yet to tell that story. The reason is simple, and you already know it. It's because my trial story is an enormous story. It is a dinosaur. It is a sinking ship. It is the Nazis. It's a harder story to tell. The story of the spoon of power, the story of my bad blood draw, those are stories that are small, relatable, and simple to tell. People love them. Someday I'm going to get around to my trial story. I haven't gotten around to it because it's just hard to tell. It requires a lot of heavy lifting. I'd rather tell the little stories first. Find your trial story. Find the one you've never gotten around to, the one you weren't sure how to tell. Start with a clear playing field if possible. Find that story. Figure out in your head exactly what happened. Think about the events that took place. Settle on one story so that as I begin to teach you how to put that story together, you can begin doing so for yourself. Find that personal story right now.
